memories.content: Recall Engine uses SQLite FTS5 index to search memories, providing fast bm25 keyword rank sorting over approved memories and candidates.
Hybrid recall search scoring combines tiered FTS keyword score (60%) and semantic similarity (25%) with project relevance (5%), recency (5%), and importance (5%).
Memory Promotion V1.1 wraps promote/archive memory update, FTS refresh, and audit log insert in one SQLite transaction to ensure database safety and rollback on failure.
Secret Redactor filters out sensitive keys such as OpenRouter sk-or-v1- keys, AWS keys, passwords, and tokens before they enter memory raw logs or embedding pipelines.
Daily curation cron job aggregates raw telemetry logs into candidate memories, filtering out redundant watcher spams.
Reality scanner computes alignment scores between actual workspace files (git state, .env configs) and CentralContext brain declarations to detect mismatches.
Clipboard telemetry watcher runs a background interval loop that intercepts copied prompt templates and parses user instructions with active telemetry.
Zsh terminal hook captures active command invocations, exit codes, and duration times directly from the interactive developer shell shell_snapshots.
Server db connection enables write-ahead logging (WAL) mode to allow concurrent read and write operations, reducing database lock contentions.
Setting a database connection busy timeout of 5000 milliseconds prevents instant SQLITE_BUSY crashes when concurrent threads update logs.
Stale memories with low importance and no recent recall counts are flagged as decay candidates, moving to quarantined archival tiers over time.
Consensus layer weights model judgements from critic, founder judge, and AI judge models to filter out low-confidence memories.
File watcher implements a 3-second debounce to avoid spams of writes during rapid sequential file saving operations.
File watcher computes SHA-256 hashes of text snapshots to verify if the file content has actually changed before invoking API payloads.
File watcher assigns Quality Score 5 (Critical) to memories and snapshots originating from planning, rules, decisions, and README files.
Configuration files such as package.json, tsconfig.json, and .env.example are scored as Quality Score 4 (High Value) snapshots.
All capture layer daemons authenticate with the CentralContext server using a secure API authorization token in x-api-key header.
Raw logs are written into daily date-stamped JSONL files, preserving an append-only absolute source of truth.
Local semantic embeddings are calculated using ONNX Runtime and the Xenova MiniLM sentence transformer model directly inside apps/server.
Embeddings computed by MiniLM are mean pooled and normalized to unit length so that cosine similarity can be calculated via simple dot product.
Inputs exceeding the 256 token limit of the local MiniLM embedding model are chunked into smaller overlap windows before embedding.
Cosine similarity maps text semantic closeness by computing the dot product of two normalized unit vectors, scoring from 0.0 to 1.0.
Context Injection computes a compact startup pack selecting top memories from recall and fits them within a 2000-token budget to avoid LLM context bloat.
Sidebar button copies a highly condensed, monospaced startup packet containing rules, state, active decisions, and top 3 parsed candidates.
Settings panel configures DeepSeek V4 Flash for final curation reviews and Qwen 3.5 Flash for classification, stored in ai_provider.json.
Token cost estimator calculates prompt expenses before making live OpenRouter API calls, enforcing strict daily budget safeguards.
All paid OpenRouter invocations write details to a daily spend ledger file, tracking actual budget consumption.
When OpenRouter API is disabled or daily budget is exhausted, curation falls back to local heuristic rules to prevent pipeline stalls.
Raw logs ingest endpoint uses content hash checks to skip writing duplicate telemetries that represent no structural changes.
Agent rules mandate context loading only during new session boots, major file edits, or architectural changes to avoid redundant loads.
AI agents must append a concise chronological work log under today's date in WORK_LOG.md upon milestone completions.
CURRENT_STATE.md serves as the ultimate spawner authority on active priority tasks and project status records.
Decay candidates are automatically tagged and archived in a low-priority quarantine vault, keeping the recall search space clean.
CLI sync utility transfers daily curated reports, approved memories, and audit logs cleanly between local machine and VPS nodes.
VSCode Cline capture daemon recursively monitors VSCode's globalStorage task folders to stream messages, command executions, and progress in real-time.
Codex capture daemon recursively watches session rollout JSONL files inside ~/.codex/sessions/ to parse prompts, tools outputs, and outputs in real-time.
Ingestion pipeline outputs redaction audits, counting scrubbed keys and passwords to document compliance statistics.
Command script recall:rebuild drops existing FTS tables and performs a complete re-indexing of all approved and candidate memories.
Reality layer outputs descriptive alignment warnings on the Dashboard if it flags inconsistencies between brain declarations and physical files.
Long-term memories located in LONG_TERM tier receive a 1.20x multiplier boost during hybrid recall search rankings.
Memories are marked as decay candidates when their promotion scores fall below 35 and have not been recalled for over 30 days.
Telemetry ingestion maps raw payload records to raw_logs table inside centralcontext.db, logging source, project, type, and content.
Critic rules evaluate proposed candidate memories, flagging logic duplicates and formatting syntax before AI curation stages.
Dashboard displays a review queue for candidate memories, allowing manual approval, rejection, or context addition by the founder.
Server Express json configuration increases payload size limit to 20MB, preventing PayloadTooLargeError crashes during large log syncs.
Memories matching the requested query project receive a full project boost score of 1.0, outranking unrelated projects during search.

memories.summary: ONNX sentence transformer MiniLM file size
memories.content: The default MiniLM L6 sentence transformer model ONNX file occupies approximately 90MB of storage resources.
Database initialization performs automatic column verification and alters raw_logs table to add content_hash, dedupe_key, and task_id if missing.
Shell installer injects centralcontext_preexec hooks into .zshrc to record interactive terminal history logs securely.
Promotion score compiles memory confidence, importance, and historical recall frequency to decide LONG_TERM upgrades.
